content_markdown: A satellite is moved from a low orbit to a higher orbit. Which of the following accurately describes the energy of the satellite?

diagram_metadata.description: A table with four rows (A, B, C, D) and three columns labeled 'Total energy', 'Gravitational potential energy', and 'Kinetic energy'.

options.label: A
options.text: Total energy: stays the same; Gravitational potential energy: decreases; Kinetic energy: increases

options.label: B
options.text: Total energy: stays the same; Gravitational potential energy: increases; Kinetic energy: decreases

options.label: C
options.text: Total energy: increases; Gravitational potential energy: decreases; Kinetic energy: increases

options.label: D
options.text: Total energy: increases; Gravitational potential energy: increases; Kinetic energy: decreases

vibe_explanation: Moving to a higher orbit requires work to be done against the gravitational field, so the total energy $E_{tot} = -\frac{GMm}{2r}$ increases (becomes less negative). Gravitational potential energy $E_p = -\frac{GMm}{r}$ also increases (becomes less negative). However, orbital speed $v = \sqrt{\frac{GM}{r}}$ decreases as radius $r$ increases, so the kinetic energy $E_k = \frac{GMm}{2r}$ decreases.
